title: thymus serpyllum subsp. serpylloides
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. serpylloides, also known as wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which can help to combat infections and soothe skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. serpylloides have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced stress and anxiety, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system function. Thyme can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb, which can be used to treat a range of ailments or added to food for its culinary and medicinal properties.
